steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "since 1945" indicates an action that started in the past and continues to the present.

steps.title: Apply the present perfect rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: the time marker "since 1945" signals an action that began in the past and continues into the present. Rule: since + past time requires present perfect tense (has/have + past participle).
  • Choice A — "is collecting": present progressive; describes an action happening now, but doesn't convey the start-in-1945-and-continues meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — "will collect": simple future; contradicts "since 1945," which marks a past starting point. ✗
  • Choice C — "has collected": present perfect; correctly conveys an action ongoing from 1945 to now. ✓
  • Choice D — "collects": simple present; expresses a habitual action but loses the "since 1945" duration. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
